content:
Course title: Differential and Integral Calculus 1
Course code: MS-A0111
University: Aalto University
Country: FI
Semester: 1
ECTS: 5
Language of source content: English
Broad category: Mathematics

Course description:
Analysis of the convergence of sequences and series, series expansions and approximations of elementary functions, properties and calculation methods of derivatives and integrals, applications of differentiation and integration, solutions to first order linear and separable differential equations, and solutions to linear second order differential equations with constant coefficients.

Main topics:
- Calculus
- Differential Equations
- Series and Sequences
- Mathematical Analysis
- Integral Calculus
- Differential Calculus

Keywords:
convergence, sequences, series, power series, elementary functions, derivative, integral, differentiation, integration, first order differential equations, linear differential equations, separable differential equations, second order differential equations, constant coefficients, series expansion

Course title: Matrix Algebra
Course code: MS-A0011
University: Aalto University
Country: FI
Semester: 1
ECTS: 5
Language of source content: English
Broad category: Mathematics

Course description:
Covers systems of linear equations in matrix form, solving them using Gaussian elimination, performing basic matrix operations, computing eigenvalues of square matrices, and understanding the significance of matrix decompositions. Includes vector computations, matrices, and systems of linear equations.

Main topics:
- Linear Algebra
- Matrix Operations
- Gaussian Elimination
- Eigenvalues
- Matrix Decomposition

Keywords:
matrix form, systems of linear equations, Gaussian elimination, matrix operations, eigenvalues, square matrix, matrix decomposition, vector computations, matrices

Course title: Differential and Integral Calculus 2
Course code: MS-A0211
University: Aalto University
Country: FI
Semester: 1
ECTS: 5
Language of source content: English
Broad category: Mathematics

Course description:
Functions of several variables and their derivatives, optimization of functions with several variables, calculation of double and triple integrals, use of partial derivatives for approximations, solving systems of equations with Newton's method, application of Lagrange multipliers, changing the order of integration in double integrals, and changing variables in double and triple integrals.

Main topics:
- Multivariable Calculus
- Partial Derivatives
- Optimization
- Multiple Integrals
- Numerical Methods

Keywords:
functions of several variables, partial derivatives, Newton's method, Lagrange multipliers, double integrals, triple integrals, order of integration, change of variables, systems of equations, optimization

Course title: First Course in Probability and Statistics
Course code: MS-A0503
University: Aalto University
Country: FI
Semester: 1
ECTS: 5
Language of source content: English
Broad category: Mathematics

Course description:
Covers the computation of probabilities for composite events using set theory, key discrete and continuous probability distributions, joint distributions of random vectors, stochastic independence, parameter estimation in statistical models, computation and interpretation of posterior distributions, and the use of statistical tests including p-values. Includes statistical data analysis with computers, expectation, variance, law of large numbers, normal approximation, correlation, data description with statistics and histograms, confidence intervals, prior and likelihood functions, and hypothesis testing.

Main topics:
- Probability Theory
- Statistical Inference
- Random Variables
- Parameter Estimation
- Hypothesis Testing
- Statistical Distributions
- Data Analysis
- Set Theory

Keywords:
probability, set theory, discrete distributions, continuous distributions, expectation, variance, law of large numbers, normal approximation, joint distributions, stochastic independence, correlation, parameter estimation, confidence interval, likelihood function, posterior distribution, p-value, hypothesis testing, histogram

metadata.title: First Course in Probability and Statistics

content:
Course title: Signals and Systems 
Course code: ELEC-A7200
University: Aalto University
Country: FI
Semester: 1
ECTS: 5
Language of source content: English
Broad category: Signal Processing

Course description:
Covers the basic principles of signal and system analysis, including common signal transforms and frequency domain representation. Topics include filtering of deterministic and stochastic signals using linear lowpass and bandpass filters, principles of analog to digital transformation, mapping parameters, nature of signals, frequency analysis, linear and memoryless systems, analysis of arbitrary signals in linear systems, and modulated signals. Includes calculation exercises and laboratory work.

Main topics:
- Signal Analysis
- System Analysis
- Frequency Domain
- Linear Systems
- Signal Filtering
- Analog to Digital Conversion
- Stochastic Signals
- Modulated Signals

Keywords:
signal transforms, frequency analysis, linear filters, lowpass filter, bandpass filter, analog to digital transform, mapping parameters, deterministic signals, stochastic signals, memoryless systems, arbitrary signals, modulated signals, signal representation, calculation exercises, laboratory work

Course title: Programming 1
Course code: CS-A1110
University: Aalto University
Country: FI
Semester: 1
ECTS: 5
Language of source content: English
Broad category: Software Engineering

Course description:
The course covers programming fundamentals including imperative and object-oriented programming, reading and writing code according to specifications, and understanding statements, expressions, variables, data types, functions, parameters, return values, objects, classes, methods, arrays, and buffers. It addresses selection, loops, programming style, class libraries, program documentation, and the use of integrated development environments. Additional topics include stages of program code from source to execution, compiler and virtual machine concepts, call stack, garbage collection, file I/O, key-value maps, traits, inheritance, immutability, function objects, higher-order and anonymous functions, code quality, graphical user interfaces, recursion, and program design.

Main topics:
- Imperative Programming
- Object-Oriented Programming
- Program Structure
- Data Types
- Control Flow
- Functions and Methods
- Inheritance
- Recursion

Keywords:
variables, statements, expressions, functions, parameters, return values, objects, classes, methods, arrays, buffers, selection, loops, compiler, virtual machine, call stack, garbage collection, file I/O, key-value maps, traits, immutability, function objects, higher-order functions, anonymous functions, integrated development environment, graphical user interface, program documentation

Course title: Programming 2
Course code: CS-A1120
University: Aalto University
Country: FI
Semester: 1
ECTS: 5
Language of source content: English
Broad category: Software Engineering

Course description:
Principles of binary representation for integers and floating point numbers, bit-level operations, and logic gates as basic operators for circuit construction such as adders. Overview of computer architecture from gate level to high-level programming. Implementation of simple functions in assembly language. Introduction to big-O notation and computational resource analysis. Exploration of functional programming, recursion, and basic parallel programming constructs.

Main topics:
- Binary Representation
- Logic Gates
- Computer Architecture
- Assembly Language
- Algorithm Analysis
- Functional Programming
- Recursion
- Parallel Programming

Keywords:
integer representation, floating point representation, bitwise operations, logic circuits, adder circuit, assembly language, big-O notation, computational resources, recursion, functional programming, parallel constructs, gate-level design, problem-solving, computer architecture

Course title: Programming 2
Course code: ELEC-A7100
University: Aalto University
Country: FI
Semester: 1
ECTS: 5
Language of source content: English
Broad category: Software Engineering

Course description:
C programming language fundamentals, including basic constructs, dynamic memory management, and standard library functions.

Main topics:
- C Programming
- Programming Fundamentals
- Dynamic Memory Management
- Standard Library Functions

Keywords:
C, pointers, malloc, free, arrays, functions, control structures, standard library, memory allocation, data types

Course title: Data Structures and Algorithms
Course code: CS-A1140
University: Aalto University
Country: FI
Semester: 1
ECTS: 5
Language of source content: English
Broad category: Software Engineering

Course description:
Principles of algorithm analysis, including elementary data structures, sorting algorithms, divide and conquer paradigm, tree data structures and algorithms, tree-based search structures, hash functions and tables, dynamic programming, graph data structures and algorithms, and multithreaded algorithms for computationally challenging problems.

Main topics:
- Algorithm Analysis
- Data Structures
- Sorting Algorithms
- Divide and Conquer
- Tree Structures
- Hashing
- Dynamic Programming
- Graph Algorithms
- Multithreaded Algorithms

Keywords:
algorithm analysis, elementary data structures, sorting, divide and conquer, tree data structures, search trees, hash functions, hash tables, dynamic programming, graph data structures, graph algorithms, multithreaded algorithms, computational complexity

Course title: Machine Learning
Course code: CS-C3240
University: Aalto University
Country: FI
Semester: 1
ECTS: 5
Language of source content: English
Broad category: Artificial Intelligence

Course description:
Formalization of applications as machine learning problems and solving them using basic machine learning methods. Exploratory data analysis, including dimensionality reduction techniques such as PCA. Application of standard regression and classification models to data sets. Implementation of clustering methods and simple dimensionality reduction. Introduction to deep learning, reinforcement learning, and language modeling.

Main topics:
- Machine Learning
- Exploratory Data Analysis
- Regression
- Classification
- Clustering
- Dimensionality Reduction
- Deep Learning
- Reinforcement Learning
- Language Modeling

Keywords:
machine learning, exploratory data analysis, regression models, classification models, clustering, dimensionality reduction, PCA, deep learning, reinforcement learning, language modeling, train-validate-test, data set, basic ML methods

Course title: Mechanics
Course code: PHYS-C0110
University: Aalto University
Country: FI
Semester: 1
ECTS: 5
Language of source content: English
Broad category: Mathematics

Course description:
Motion, force, momentum, kinetic energy, angular momentum, rotational energy, and moment of inertia. Inertial and non-inertial coordinate systems, fictitious forces, Galilean relativity principle, and Galilean transformation. Periodic motion, damped oscillation, and fundamental concepts of wave motion.

Main topics:
- Classical Mechanics
- Rotational Dynamics
- Oscillations
- Wave Motion
- Reference Frames
- Relativity

Keywords:
motion, force, momentum, kinetic energy, angular momentum, moment of inertia, rotational energy, inertial coordinate system, non-inertial coordinate system, fictitious force, Galilean relativity, Galilean transformation, periodic motion, damped oscillation, wave motion

Course title: Computer Architectures with RISC-V 
Course code: ELEC-C7440
University: Aalto University
Country: FI
Semester: 1
ECTS: 5
Language of source content: English
Broad category: Hardware & Architecture

Course description:
The course covers the evolution of computer architecture, focusing on the design factors of hardware and software elements in computer systems. It examines common architectural components and the internal operation of modern digital computers, with attention to trade-offs at the hardware-software interface.

Main topics:
- Computer Architecture
- Hardware-Software Interface
- Digital Systems
- Instruction Set Architecture
- System Design

Keywords:
RISC-V, processor design, hardware design, software design, architectural trade-offs, digital computer systems, instruction set, system components, hardware-software interaction, modern architectures

Course title: Basic Principles in Networking 
Course code: ELEC-C7420
University: Aalto University
Country: FI
Semester: 1
ECTS: 5
Language of source content: English
Broad category: Networking

Course description:
This course covers theoretical and practical foundations of computer networking, including principles of network communication and detailed case studies of protocols across the OSI model layers. Topics include HTTP and TCP/IP for data center networking, Bluetooth and Bluetooth Low Energy for short-range communication, and symmetric and asymmetric encryption protocols as well as VPNs for secure communications.

Main topics:
- Computer Networking
- OSI Model
- Network Protocols
- Data Center Networking
- Short-Range Communication
- Network Security

Keywords:
HTTP, TCP/IP, Bluetooth, Bluetooth Low Energy, Symmetric Encryption, Asymmetric Encryption, VPN, Network Protocols, OSI Layers, Data Center, Device-to-Device Communication, Secure Communication, Encryption Protocols

Course title: Basics of Electronic Circuits
Course code: ELEC-C9620
University: Aalto University
Country: FI
Semester: 1
ECTS: 5
Language of source content: English
Broad category: Hardware & Architecture

Course description:
Quantities, definitions, and laws in electronics. Basic circuit components including resistor, inductor, and capacitor. Analysis methods for direct-current and alternating-current circuits. Frequency-domain representation and transfer functions. Nonlinearity in electronics. Introduction to operational amplifiers.

Main topics:
- Electronic Circuits
- Circuit Analysis
- Frequency Domain
- Operational Amplifiers
- Nonlinear Electronics

Keywords:
resistor, inductor, capacitor, DC analysis, AC analysis, transfer function, frequency response, nonlinearity, operational amplifier, Ohm's Law, Kirchhoff's Laws, impedance, circuit components

Course title: Information Security
Course code: CS-C3130
University: Aalto University
Country: FI
Semester: 1
ECTS: 5
Language of source content: en
Broad category: Computer Science

Course description:
This course provides a comprehensive introduction to information security. Topics include security models and terminology, authentication, access control, software security, cryptography, network security, threat analysis, identity management, privacy, and security policies and regulation. After completing the course, students will be familiar with the key concepts and abstractions of information security and understand the purpose, function, and weaknesses of several security technologies. They will be able to model threats and critically analyze the security of a system from an attacker’s point of view.

Main topics:
- Information security fundamentals
- Security models and terminology
- Authentication
- Access control
- Software security
- Cryptography
- Network security
- Threat modeling and analysis
- Identity management
- Privacy
- Security policies and regulation
- Attacker mindset and adversarial thinking

Keywords:
information security, security models, authentication, access control, software security, cryptography, network security, threat analysis, threat modeling, identity management, privacy, security policies, security regulation, system security, attacker perspective

Course title: Software Engineering
Course code: CS-C3150
University: Aalto University
Country: FI
Semester: 1
ECTS: 5
Language of source content: en
Broad category: Computer Science

Course description:
The course covers the main problems, models, and methods of software engineering, including both traditional and agile/lean software development approaches. It addresses the core software engineering activities: requirements engineering, software architecture, software design, implementation, testing, and deployment. In addition, the course discusses supporting workflows such as configuration management and project management.

Main topics:
- Software engineering fundamentals
- Software development life cycle
- Traditional software development models
- Agile and lean software development
- Requirements engineering
- Software architecture
- Software design
- Software implementation
- Software testing
- Software deployment
- Configuration management
- Project management in software engineering

Keywords:
software engineering, software development, agile, lean, waterfall, software process models, requirements engineering, software requirements, software architecture, software design, implementation, coding, software testing, quality assurance, deployment, release management, configuration management, version control, project management, software projects

Course title: Human-Computer Interaction
Course code: CS-C3120
University: Aalto University
Country: FI
Semester: 1
ECTS: 5
Language of source content: en
Broad category: Computer Science

Course description:
This course introduces the foundations of Human-Computer Interaction (HCI), focusing on how digital systems and services can be designed to support the needs of their intended users. Students learn the basics of user interface (UI) and user experience (UX) design, with an emphasis on the human-centred design process for understanding user needs and requirements and for testing design solutions. Core topics include usability, human-centred design principles, prototyping methods, and how HCI practices integrate into contemporary software product development processes.

Main topics:
- Human-Computer Interaction fundamentals
- User interface (UI) design
- User experience (UX) design
- Human-centred design process
- User needs and requirements analysis
- Usability principles and evaluation
- Prototyping techniques
- User testing and feedback
- Integration of HCI in software product development

Keywords:
Human-Computer Interaction, HCI, user interface, UI design, user experience, UX design, human-centred design, user needs, requirements analysis, usability, usability testing, prototyping, user testing, software product development, interaction design

metadata.title: Human-Computer Interaction
topics: Integration of HCI in software product development

content:
Course title: Computer Graphics
Course code: CS-C3100
University: Aalto University
Country: FI
Semester: 1
ECTS: 5
Language of source content: en
Broad category: Computer Science

Course description:
This course provides a basic understanding of modern computer graphics as an integrated field, covering modeling, animation, and rendering. Students will learn to implement practical graphics algorithms in C++ and gain insight into how graphics processors (GPUs) work. Topics include geometric modelling, visual simulation, shading and lighting, ray tracing, the real-time graphics pipeline, and the mathematical foundations of computer graphics.

Main topics:
- Geometric modelling
- Animation
- Rendering
- Visual simulation
- Shading and lighting
- Ray tracing
- Real-time graphics pipeline
- GPU architecture and operation
- Mathematical foundations of computer graphics
- C++ implementation of graphics algorithms

Keywords:
computer graphics, geometric modelling, animation, rendering, visual simulation, shading, lighting, ray tracing, real-time rendering, graphics pipeline, GPU, graphics processor, C++, graphics algorithms, mathematical foundations

Course title: Operating Systems
Course code: CS-C3140
University: Aalto University
Country: FI
Semester: 1
ECTS: 5
Language of source content: en
Broad category: Computer Science

Course description:
After completing this course, students will be able to explain the principles behind the implementation of operating systems, discuss abstraction and management of system resources, and describe the typical data structures and algorithms commonly employed in operating systems. The course covers operating system structure and elements of computer architecture, processes and threads, memory management and file systems, concurrency and parallelism, and virtualization.

Main topics:
- Operating system structure
- Computer architecture fundamentals for OS
- Processes and threads
- CPU scheduling
- Memory management
- Virtual memory
- File systems
- Input/output management
- Concurrency
- Parallelism
- Synchronization
- Deadlocks
- Virtualization

Keywords:
operating systems, OS implementation, system resources, resource management, processes, threads, scheduling, memory management, virtual memory, file systems, I/O systems, concurrency, parallelism, synchronization, mutex, semaphores, deadlock, virtualization, computer architecture, kernel, system calls

Course title: Databases
Course code: CS-A1150
University: Aalto University
Country: FI
Semester: 1
ECTS: 5
Language of source content: en
Broad category: Computer Science

Course description:
The course provides an introduction to relational database systems and their use. Topics include database design using the Entity-Relationship model, the relational data model, relational algebra, SQL querying and updates, application programming with databases, transaction management, and basic database architecture including indexing and query execution.

Main topics:
- Relational database systems
- Database design
- Entity-Relationship model
- Relational data model
- Relational algebra
- SQL querying
- SQL updates
- Application programming with databases
- Transaction management
- Database architecture
- Indexing
- Query execution

Keywords:
relational databases, database design, ER model, relational model, relational algebra, SQL, queries, updates, database applications, transactions, ACID properties, database architecture, indexes, query processing, query optimization

Course title: Web Software Development
Course code: CS-C3170
University: Aalto University
Country: FI
Semester: 1
ECTS: 5
Language of source content: en
Broad category: Computer Science

Course description:
This course introduces the principles and practice of modern web software development. Students learn what web applications are, how they function, and how they are constructed. The course covers the responsibilities and interaction of client-side and server-side web applications, and guides students in designing, implementing, and testing complete web applications. Core topics include the Internet and HTTP, HTML, CSS, JavaScript, web development tools, server- and client-side architectures, working with databases, containerization and testing, managing web application state, authentication, APIs, and web application security.

Main topics:
- Web application architecture
- Client-side web development
- Server-side web development
- Internet and HTTP
- HTML and semantic markup
- CSS and styling
- JavaScript programming
- Web development tools and workflows
- Databases and data persistence
- Containerization
- Software testing for web applications
- State management in web applications
- Authentication and authorization
- API design and integration
- Web application security

Keywords:
web development, web applications, client-side, server-side, HTTP, HTML, CSS, JavaScript, frontend, backend, web frameworks, databases, SQL, containerization, Docker, testing, unit testing, integration testing, state management, authentication, authorization, APIs, REST, web security, XSS, CSRF, session management, full-stack development

Course title: Complex Networks
Course code: CS-E5740
University: Aalto University
Country: FI
Semester: 1
ECTS: 5
Language of source content: en
Broad category: Computer Science

Course description:
This course introduces the fundamentals of complex network theory and provides practical skills for analyzing real-world networks. Students will learn about stochastic interactions, non-trivial interaction structures, core network science algorithms, and their applications in areas such as computational social science and biological networks.

Main topics:
- Complex network theory
- Network models and structures
- Stochastic interactions in networks
- Non-trivial interaction structures
- Network science algorithms
- Computational social science applications
- Biological network analysis

Keywords:
complex networks, network theory, network analysis, stochastic interactions, network structure, network algorithms, computational social science, biological networks, network science

Course title: Advanced Course in Algorithms
Course code: CS-E4500
University: Aalto University
Country: FI
Semester: 1
ECTS: 5
Language of source content: en
Broad category: Computer Science / Algorithms

Course description:
This course deepens students’ knowledge and skills in algorithm design, with a focus on advanced design principles and the tradeoffs between solution quality, running time, and other computational resources. Covered topics include randomized algorithms, approximation algorithms, parameterized algorithms, and algebraic techniques in algorithm design, along with contemporary advanced algorithms and their supporting data structures.

Main topics:
- Advanced algorithm design
- Randomized algorithms
- Approximation algorithms
- Parameterized algorithms
- Algebraic techniques in algorithms
- Algorithmic tradeoffs and complexity
- Advanced data structures
- Contemporary algorithmic applications

Keywords:
advanced algorithms, algorithm design, randomization, randomized algorithms, approximation, approximation algorithms, parameterisation, parameterized algorithms, algebrisation, algebraic algorithms, computational resources, time complexity, space complexity, data structures

Course title: Logic and Hard Computational Problems
Course code: CS-E4700
University: Aalto University
Country: FI
Semester: 1
ECTS: 5
Language of source content: en
Broad category: Computer Science

Course description:
This course introduces the logical formalization of mathematical reasoning and the mathematics of computational intractability. Students learn to work with and reason using propositional logic and first-order logic. The course covers techniques for proving that one computational problem is at least as hard as another via efficient reductions. It introduces the complexity classes P and NP, and focuses on the hardest problems in NP, namely NP-complete problems.

Main topics:
- Propositional logic
- First-order logic
- Mathematical reasoning
- Computational complexity
- Complexity classes P and NP
- NP-completeness
- Reductions between computational problems
- Computational intractability
- Decision problems
- Formal logic in computer science

Keywords:
propositional logic, first-order logic, logical formalization, mathematical reasoning, computational intractability, computational complexity, complexity theory, P, NP, NP-complete, NP-completeness, reductions, polynomial-time reduction, hardness, decision problems

Course title: Mobile Systems Programming
Course code: CS-E4170
University: Aalto University
Country: FI
Semester: 1
ECTS: 5
Language of source content: en
Broad category: Computer Science

Course description:
This course provides an overview of mobile computing platforms, mobile application development, and mobile service design. Covered topics include smartphone operating system architectures, sensor data collection, user interface design, persistent data storage, background processing, and communication in mobile systems. Students will design and implement a mobile application as a project.

Main topics:
- Mobile computing platforms
- Mobile application development
- Mobile service design
- Smartphone operating system architectures
- Sensor data collection
- Mobile user interfaces
- Persistent data storage on mobile devices
- Background processing in mobile systems
- Communication in mobile systems
- Project-based mobile app development

Keywords:
mobile systems programming, mobile computing, mobile applications, smartphone OS, Android, iOS, sensor data, mobile UI, persistent storage, background services, mobile networking, mobile communication, app development project

metadata.title: Mobile Systems Programming
topics: Persistent data storage on mobile devices

content:
Course title: Music Programming
Course code: CS-E2222
University: Aalto University
Country: FI
Semester: 1
ECTS: 5
Language of source content: en
Broad category: Computer Science / Music Technology

Course description:
Music Programming covers sine wave representations, frequency analysis, timbre specification, and the fundamentals of creating music through programming, with an emphasis on exploratory and recreational coding practices.

Main topics:
- Sine wave representations
- Frequency analysis
- Timbre specification
- Algorithmic music creation
- Music programming fundamentals
- Recreational and exploratory coding in music

Keywords:
music programming, sine waves, frequency analysis, timbre, sound synthesis, algorithmic composition, creative coding, audio programming

metadata.title: Music Programming
topics: Recreational and exploratory coding in music

content:
Course title: Robotics and Autonomous Systems
Course code: ELEC-E8125
University: Aalto University
Country: FI
Semester: 2
ECTS: 5
Language of source content: en
Broad category: Robotics and Autonomous Systems

Course description:
Introduction to robotic perception, motion planning, sensor fusion, autonomous navigation, and control systems for mobile robots.

Main topics:
- robotic perception
- motion planning
- sensor fusion
- autonomous navigation
- control systems
- mobile robots

Keywords:
robotics, autonomous systems, robotic perception, motion planning, sensor fusion, autonomous navigation, control systems, mobile robots

Course title: Deep Learning
Course code: CS-E4890
University: Aalto University
Country: FI
Semester: 3
ECTS: 5
Language of source content: en
Broad category: Computer Science

Course description:
This course provides an introduction to deep learning, covering core neural network architectures, backpropagation, convolutional neural networks, recurrent neural networks, transformers, and their applications in computer vision and natural language processing.

Main topics:
- Neural network architectures
- Backpropagation
- Convolutional neural networks
- Recurrent neural networks
- Transformers
- Computer vision
- Natural language processing

Keywords:
deep learning, neural networks, backpropagation, convolutional networks, CNN, recurrent networks, RNN, transformers, computer vision, natural language processing, NLP
